question.text: Which of the following is TRUE regarding SEBI SCORES (SEBI Complaint Redress System)?

answer.text: Only investors with investments above Rs. 10 lakh can file complaints on SCORES
SCORES can only be used after filing a police complaint
It is an online platform where investors can lodge, track, and view resolution of their complaints against SEBI-registered entities
SCORES automatically awards monetary compensation to the investor

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI SCORES is a web-based centralized grievance redressal system. Any investor can lodge a complaint online, track its progress, and view the resolution. SEBI-registered entities (including AMCs, distributors, RTAs) are required to respond to complaints on SCORES within 30 days.
**The Trap:** SCORES facilitates complaint resolution but does not automatically award compensation. It is not a court or arbitration body. If the investor is not satisfied with the resolution on SCORES, they can escalate to the Online Dispute Resolution (ODR) mechanism or approach appropriate legal forums.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Before approaching SCORES, investors should first try to resolve the complaint directly with the AMC/entity. SCORES is the second level of escalation. The entire process is online, making it accessible to investors across the country.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Treat a complaint on SCORES as a serious matter — it creates a regulatory record. Proactively resolve client issues before they reach SCORES. If a complaint is filed, cooperate fully and respond within the 30-day timeline. A pattern of SCORES complaints can invite SEBI scrutiny of your distribution practices.
